baseline.notes: Run 50 sporting_ac with field_crop (no upscale): F1 0.210, P 0.515, R 0.132, TP 17/129, FP 16. Coarse firing rate 6.5% vs Rush 30%. Run 51 adds LANCZOS upscale of the cropped image to long-edge=1920 (1280x~540 -> 1920x~810), keeping same field content but giving the VLM more 28x28 patches over it. Tests whether attention capacity on cropped images is the bottleneck.

targets.notes: If upscale is net positive, F1 climbs meaningfully. If ~flat, we've learned the VLM isn't bottlenecked by visual-token count — move to Run 52 (ball-centric crops) which would actually add new information via YOLO-driven framing.

field_crop_upscale_long_edge.reason: Upscale each field crop so its long edge = 1440 via LANCZOS. First attempt at 1920 busted max_model_len=8192 (Qwen3-VL emits ~1972 tokens per 1920x816 image, 5 images = ~9860 > limit). Recalculated budget: 1440x~612 crop = ~1071 tokens per image, 5 * 1071 = 5355 + prompt 600 + output 800 = 6755, fits with margin.

notes: Run #51 (resubmitted at upscale=1440): first submission at upscale=1920 (job 0b84e055) busted max_model_len=8192 with every window returning HTTP 400 'Input length 9309 exceeds 8192'. My token math was wrong — Qwen3-VL does not apply the 2x2 merge I assumed. Lowered upscale target to 1440, resubmitted as job 0adfc8ac. Pre-flight verified zero api_errors since restart.
